title: Successful PIN login shows module launcher menu
description: Verifies that selecting a user and entering a correct 4-digit PIN logs in and displays the module selection menu.

steps.description: Click on the first user in the user list

title: Incorrect PIN shows invalid feedback and resets PIN entry
description: Verifies that entering an incorrect 4-digit PIN shows an invalid PIN message and clears the PIN input for retry.

steps.description: Click on the first user in the user list

title: PIN entry blocks access until 4 digits are entered
description: Verifies that the module selection menu does not appear after fewer than 4 PIN digits are entered.

steps.description: Click on the first user in the user list

title: Switching selected user clears any in-progress PIN entry
description: Verifies that changing the selected user resets the PIN entry state so digits from a previous selection are not carried over.

steps.description: Click on the first user in the user list
Click on the second user in the user list

title: Invalid PIN feedback disappears after retrying PIN entry
description: Verifies that after an invalid PIN attempt, starting a new PIN entry removes/does not persist the invalid feedback while retrying.

steps.description: Click on the first user in the user list
Verify text "Invalid PIN" is not visible

title: PIN keypad input is visible as masked PIN indicators
description: Verifies that each keypad press updates visible PIN indicators (masked) to reflect the number of digits entered.

steps.description: Click on the first user in the user list

title: Open POS module from launcher after successful PIN login
description: Verifies that an authenticated operator can see module cards and open the POS module from the launcher.

steps.description: Click on an available user profile on the login screen

title: Module cards are visible on launcher after authentication
description: Verifies the launcher lists all expected module cards after the operator authenticates.

steps.description: Click on an available user profile on the login screen

title: Admin access denied for non-admin user from launcher
description: Verifies that a non-admin operator cannot open the Admin module and stays on the launcher with an access denied message.

steps.description: Click on a non-admin user profile on the login screen

title: Return to launcher from POS using 'Trocar Módulo'
description: Verifies the operator can return from the POS module back to the launcher using the module switch control.

steps.description: Click on an available user profile on the login screen

title: Logout from launcher returns to login screen
description: Verifies that logging out from the launcher returns the user to the login screen.

steps.description: Click on an available user profile on the login screen

title: Invalid PIN does not allow access to launcher
description: Verifies that entering an incorrect PIN keeps the operator on the login screen and shows an error message.

steps.description: Click on an available user profile on the login screen

title: Attempt to open multiple modules sequentially via launcher (POS -> back -> KDS)
description: Verifies module switching works repeatedly by opening POS, returning to launcher, then opening KDS.

steps.description: Click on an available user profile on the login screen

title: Launcher remains usable after admin access denial
description: Verifies that after an access denied attempt on Admin, the operator can still open an allowed module (POS).

steps.description: Click on a non-admin user profile on the login screen

title: Complete POS sale with cash payment and success confirmation
description: Verifies a cashier can open a shift, add an item to cart, take a cash payment, and see an order success confirmation.

steps.description: Click the "POS" module from the launcher
Verify text "Open cashier shift" is visible
Type "Test Operator" into the operator name field
Click on a product from the product list
Verify the selected product name is visible in the cart
Select "Cash" from the payment method dropdown

title: Search or filter products and add to cart
description: Verifies product search/filter narrows results and cashier can add a searched item to the cart.

steps.description: Click the "POS" module from the launcher
Type "Test Operator" into the operator name field
Click into the product search field
Type "Burger" into the product search field
Click a visible product result that matches the search
Verify the selected product name is visible in the cart

title: Adjust item quantity in cart before checkout
description: Verifies cashier can increase an item quantity in the cart and see the quantity update before paying.

steps.description: Click the "POS" module from the launcher
Type "Test Operator" into the operator name field
Click on a product from the product list
Verify the product name is visible in the cart
Click the "+" (increase quantity) control for the cart item

title: Add modifiers to a cart item and verify they appear in cart
description: Verifies cashier can open item details/modifiers, select a modifier, and see the modifier reflected in the cart.

steps.description: Click the "POS" module from the launcher
Type "Test Operator" into the operator name field
Click on a product from the product list
Click the cart item to open item details/modifiers
Click a modifier option (for example, "Add Cheese")
Verify the chosen modifier text is visible under the cart item

title: Validation error when attempting checkout without an open shift, then open shift and retry
description: Verifies POS blocks checkout without an open shift, shows the expected validation message, then allows checkout after opening a shift.

steps.description: Click the "POS" module from the launcher
Click on a product from the product list
Verify text "Open a shift first" is visible
Type "Test Operator" into the operator name field

title: Card payment declined shows error and allows retry
description: Verifies a declined card payment displays a visible error and cashier can return to payment to retry.

steps.description: Click the "POS" module from the launcher
Type "Test Operator" into the operator name field
Click on a product from the product list
Select "Card" from the payment method dropdown

title: Cancel payment modal returns to cart without placing order
description: Verifies cashier can open the payment modal then cancel/close it and remain on the cart screen with items intact.

steps.description: Click the "POS" module from the launcher
Type "Test Operator" into the operator name field
Click on a product from the product list
Verify the product name is visible in the cart
Verify the product name is visible in the cart

title: After successful sale, continue next sale resets cart
description: Verifies that after a successful payment, cashier can continue to next sale and the cart is cleared for a new order.

steps.description: Click the "POS" module from the launcher
Type "Test Operator" into the operator name field
Click on a product from the product list
Select "Cash" from the payment method dropdown
Verify text "Cart is empty" is visible

title: KDS: Advance an order from PAID to PREPARING to READY
description: Verifies a kitchen user can select a paid/pending order and bump it through PREPARING to READY with visible status updates.

steps.description: Click on "KDS" in the module launcher
Verify a list of orders is visible
Click on the first order in the list that shows status "PAID" or "PENDING"
Click on "Bump" to move the order to "PREPARING"
Verify text "PREPARING" is visible for the selected order
Click on "Bump" to move the order to "READY"
Verify text "READY" is visible for the selected order

title: KDS: Verify realtime UI update occurs after bumping an order status
description: Verifies that after changing an order status, the KDS order list reflects the new status without a manual refresh action.

steps.description: Click on "KDS" in the module launcher
Verify a list of orders is visible
Click on the first order in the list that shows status "PAID" or "PENDING"
Click on "Bump" to move the order to "PREPARING"
Verify the selected order shows status "PREPARING" in the order list
Click on the KDS header area or an empty area of the page
Verify the order still shows status "PREPARING" in the order list

title: KDS: View PREPARING orders with visible SLA timers
description: Verifies that PREPARING orders display an SLA timer/age indicator that is visible in the KDS list.

steps.description: Click on "KDS" in the module launcher
Verify a list of orders is visible
Scroll to the section that contains orders with status "PREPARING"
Verify at least one "PREPARING" status label is visible
Verify an SLA timer or time indicator is visible near a "PREPARING" order

title: KDS: Mark an order item as PARTIAL for partial delivery
description: Verifies a kitchen user can mark part of an order as PARTIAL and see the partial state reflected visually on the order.

steps.description: Click on "KDS" in the module launcher
Verify a list of orders is visible
Click on the first order in the list that shows status "PREPARING"
Verify the order details panel is visible
Click on the first order item in the order details list
Verify text "PARTIAL" is visible for the selected item or order

title: KDS: PARTIAL state is visible on downstream modules (TV board) via realtime update
description: Verifies that after setting PARTIAL on KDS, the TV module reflects the updated state without relying on direct URL deep-linking.

steps.description: Click on "KDS" in the module launcher
Verify a list of orders is visible
Click on the first order in the list that shows status "PREPARING"
Click on "Partial" or "Mark Partial" in the order details
Verify text "PARTIAL" is visible for the order
Click on "Home" or "Launcher" to return to the module launcher
Click on "TV" in the module launcher
Verify text "TV" or "Status Board" is visible
Verify text "PARTIAL" is visible for the same order number (or the most recently updated order)

title: KDS: Recall a bumped order back to a previous state (if supported)
description: Verifies that an order can be recalled (un-bumped) and the UI reflects the prior status after the recall action.

steps.description: Click on "KDS" in the module launcher
Verify a list of orders is visible
Click on the first order in the list that shows status "READY"
Verify the order details panel is visible
Verify text "PREPARING" or "PAID" is visible for the selected order

title: Complete kiosk order from splash to receipt with customization and payment
description: Verifies a customer can start the kiosk, add a customized item to the cart, complete payment selection/confirmation, and see a success or receipt screen.

steps.description: Click on the Splash screen start button (or the main Splash screen area) to begin ordering
Click on a category in the category list
Click on a product tile in the product grid
Type "No onions" into the notes field
Click on the cart button to open the cart
Click on a visible payment method option

title: Start ordering flow and verify category browsing updates product grid
description: Verifies that selecting categories after starting the kiosk updates the visible menu browsing state.

steps.description: Click on the Splash screen start button (or the main Splash screen area) to begin ordering
Click on a first category in the category list
Click on a different category in the category list

title: Add multiple items to cart and verify cart summary reflects additions
description: Verifies that multiple items can be added and the cart view reflects the added line items before payment.

steps.description: Click on the Splash screen start button (or the main Splash screen area) to begin ordering
Click on a product tile in the product grid
Click on another product tile in the product grid
Click on the cart button to open the cart

title: Payment method selection is required before confirming payment
description: Verifies the kiosk prevents confirming payment without selecting a payment method (when multiple methods are presented).

steps.description: Click on the Splash screen start button (or the main Splash screen area) to begin ordering
Click on a product tile in the product grid
Click on the cart button to open the cart
Verify text "Select payment method" is visible

title: Back out from cart to menu and continue shopping
description: Verifies the customer can open the cart, return to menu browsing, and add another item without losing the session state.

steps.description: Click on the Splash screen start button (or the main Splash screen area) to begin ordering
Click on a product tile in the product grid
Click on the cart button to open the cart
Click on another product tile in the product grid

title: Start kiosk, open a product, and verify customization controls are available
description: Verifies that opening a product presents modifier selection and a notes input before adding to cart.

steps.description: Click on the Splash screen start button (or the main Splash screen area) to begin ordering
Click on a product tile in the product grid
Click on a modifier option (if visible) to toggle/select it
Type "Extra spicy" into the notes field

title: TV board shows expected sections with populated orders
description: Verifies the TV status board renders the two main sections and displays at least one order card when orders exist.

steps.description: Verify element "an order card in the 'Prontos' section" is visible
Verify element "an order card in the 'Em Preparo' section" is visible

title: TV board shows empty state when there are no orders
description: Verifies the TV status board displays the empty-state message when no orders are available.

steps.description: Verify text "Nenhum pedido no momento" is visible
Verify element "any order card" is not visible

title: Overdue/SLA-breached orders are highlighted
description: Verifies the board visually highlights overdue orders to draw attention to SLA breaches.

steps.description: Verify element "an order card marked as overdue (SLA breach highlight)" is visible
Verify element "a visible overdue indicator (e.g., warning label/priority highlight) on that order" is visible

title: Empty state persists and does not crash while waiting for updates
description: Verifies the screen remains stable on the empty state while waiting for polling/realtime updates.

steps.description: Verify text "Nenhum pedido no momento" is visible
Verify text "Nenhum pedido no momento" is visible

title: Create a new user (or scout) record from Admin and verify it appears in the list
description: Verifies an admin can create a new record with a role and see it persisted in the Admin list with a success confirmation.

steps.description: Click on "Admin" in the module launcher
Click on the "Users" section (or "Scouts" section if Users is not available)
Type "Auto User A" into the name field
Type "auto.user.a@example.test" into the email/identifier field (if present)
Select "Admin" (or any available non-empty role) from the Role dropdown
Verify text "Auto User A" is visible

title: Validation when creating a user/scout with missing required fields
description: Verifies required-field validation prevents saving and shows a visible error message.

steps.description: Click on "Admin" in the module launcher
Click on the "Users" section (or "Scouts" section if Users is not available)

title: Close store session is blocked when a shift is open (shows validation error)
description: Verifies the Admin store session control prevents closing a session when at least one shift is open and displays the expected validation message.

steps.description: Click on "Admin" in the module launcher
Click on the "Store Control" or "Store Session" section
Verify text "Cannot close session with open shifts" is visible

title: Resolve open shifts from Admin and close store session successfully
description: Verifies an admin can resolve open shifts and then successfully close the store session with a visible success confirmation.

steps.description: Click on "Admin" in the module launcher
Click on the "Store Control" or "Store Session" section
Click the "Resolve Open Shifts" button (or "Close Shift" for the listed open shift)
Verify text "No open shifts" is visible

title: Edit product availability in Admin and verify it updates visibly in POS
description: Verifies a product availability change saved in Admin is reflected in the POS UI when viewing the product list.

steps.description: Click on "Admin" in the module launcher
Click on the first product in the list to open its details editor
Click the "Availability" toggle (or similar in-stock/available control) to change its state
Click on "POS" in the module launcher

title: Edit promotion validity dates in Admin and verify it appears updated in Kiosk promotions view
description: Verifies a promotion validity change saved in Admin is visible in the Kiosk UI (as available/unavailable or updated label).

steps.description: Click on "Admin" in the module launcher
Click on the first promotion in the list to open its editor
Type "2099-12-31" into the promotion "Valid until" field (or equivalent end date field)
Click on "Kiosk" in the module launcher

title: Cancel out of an edit form without saving does not persist changes
description: Verifies that leaving an edit/create screen via Cancel/Back does not apply changes to the list view.

steps.description: Click on "Admin" in the module launcher
Click on the first product in the list to open its details editor
Type "TEMP NAME CHANGE" into the product name field
Verify text "TEMP NAME CHANGE" is not visible

title: Delete a record from Admin and verify it is removed from the list
description: Verifies an admin can delete a manageable record (product/promotion/scout/user) and sees confirmation and list removal.

steps.description: Click on "Admin" in the module launcher
Click on the "Promotions" section (or "Products" if Promotions is not available)
Type "Auto Promo To Delete" into the promotion name field
Verify text "Auto Promo To Delete" is visible
Click on "Auto Promo To Delete" in the list to open details
Verify text "Auto Promo To Delete" is not visible
